2604.07988.title: LogAct: Enabling Agentic Reliability via Shared Logs
2604.07988.paper_summary: Agents are LLM-driven components that can mutate environments in powerful, arbitrary ways. Extracting guarantees for the execution of agents in production environments can be challenging due to asynchrony and failures. In this paper, we propose a new abstraction called LogAct, where each agent is a deconstructed state machine playing a shared log. In LogAct, agentic actions are visible in the shared log before they are executed; can be stopped prior to execution by pluggable, decoupled voters; and recovered consistently in the case of agent or environment failure. LogAct enables agentic introspection, allowing the agent to analyze its own execution history using LLM inference, which in turn enables semantic variants of recovery, health check, and optimization. In our evaluation, LogAct agents recover efficiently and correctly from failures; debug their own performance; optimize token usage in swarms; and stop all unwanted actions for a target model on a representative benchmark with just a 3% drop in benign utility.
2604.07988.paper_summary_zh: 代理是由LLM驅動的組件，可以以強大且任意的方式改變環境。由於非同步性和故障，為代理在生產環境中的執行提取保證可能具有挑戰性。在本文中，我們提出了一種新的抽象稱為LogAct，其中每個代理都是一個解構的狀態機，播放共享日誌。在LogAct中，代理的行動在執行之前可在共享日誌中可見；可以通過可插拔的、解耦的投票者在執行之前停止；並且在代理或環境故障的情況下可以一致地恢復。LogAct使代理能夠進行自我內省，允許代理使用LLM推理分析其自身的執行歷史，這反過來又使恢復、健康檢查和優化的語義變體成為可能。在我們的評估中，LogAct代理能有效且正確地從故障中恢復；調試自己的性能；在群體中優化標記使用；並且在一個代表性基準上，僅以3%的良性效用下降停止所有不必要的行動。
2604.07988.author: Mahesh Balakrishnan et.al.
2604.07988.authors: Mahesh Balakrishnan, Ashwin Bharambe, Davide Testuggine, David Geraghty, David Mao, Vidhya Venkat, Ilya Mironov, Rithesh Baradi, Gayathri Aiyer, Victoria Dudin
